title: Buyer-Seller Messaging
description: Buyers and sellers exchange messages in typed threads (inquiry, direct, offer, custom request) with realtime delivery, typing indicators, read receipts, online status, safety filtering, unread tracking, and archiving.

flows.title: Open Messaging
flows.trigger: User opens inbox or clicks Message on a listing

steps.title: Open Messaging
steps.notes: Entry point for all messaging flows. Accessible from the nav inbox badge or from a listing detail page CTA. Route is /messages — auth-guarded by proxy.ts.

steps.title: Show Loading Skeleton
steps.tooltip: Renders 5 skeleton rows with role="status" and sr-only "Loading your messages" while useThreads() is pending. Each skeleton row has avatar, body, and timestamp placeholders.

steps.title: Fetch Thread List
steps.tooltip: Returns all threads where user is a participant, joined with participant member details and my_unread_count. Optional ?type= filter accepts: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request. Client fetches all threads then filters client-side by active tab.

steps.title: Fetch Unread Count
steps.tooltip: Sum of unread_count across all active participant rows for this user. Drives the inbox badge in the navigation header. Query key: ['messaging', 'unread-count']. No polling — invalidated by useRealtimeUnreadCount on participant row changes.

steps.title: Show Error State
steps.tooltip: ErrorState banner: "Something went wrong loading your messages" with a Retry button that calls refetch(). Shown when useThreads() isError is true.

steps.title: Render Filter Tabs
steps.tooltip: Tabs component with five tabs: All, Inquiries, Offers, Custom Requests, Direct. Each tab shows its unread count badge (summed from my_unread_count across filtered threads). Switching tabs filters allThreads client-side by thread.type — no additional API call.

steps.title: Render Thread List
steps.tooltip: ThreadList renders a <ul> of ThreadRow components. Each ThreadRow links to /messages/{thread_id} and shows: other participant avatar, name (bold if unread), TypeBadge (colored pill for thread type), last_message_preview truncated, relative timestamp (Just now / Xm ago / Xh ago / Yesterday / MMM D), and an unread dot indicator. When last_message_preview equals 'Sent a photo' (IMAGE_MESSAGE_PREVIEW constant), a HiCamera icon is prepended to the preview text.

steps.title: Show Empty State
steps.tooltip: Empty state with chat icon, "No messages yet" heading, tab-contextual description (e.g. "You have no offers yet" on the Offers tab), and a "Browse listings" CTA that routes to /search. Shown when the filtered thread list has zero items.

paths.label: No — filtered empty
paths.detail: Tab selected but no matching threads

flows.title: Start a New Thread
flows.trigger: Buyer clicks Message on a listing detail page, or member initiates direct message

steps.title: Create Thread
steps.tooltip: Body: { type, participantIds, roles, listingId?, shopId? }. Returns 201 for new thread, 409 if a thread with the same participants and type already exists (client should redirect to the existing thread). type must be one of: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request. listingId required for inquiry; shopId required for custom_request. Any authenticated member can create a direct thread with any other member — no prior transaction required. A bidirectional block check returns 403 if either member has blocked the other.

errorCases.condition: roles length does not match participantIds
A block relationship exists between the two members (either direction)
errorCases.result: Returns 403 — You cannot message this user
Returns 409 — client redirects to existing thread

steps.title: Show Block Error
steps.notes: On 403 from POST /api/messaging/threads: the block check prevents thread creation when either member has blocked the other. The generic error toast in MessageButton displays 'Something went wrong — Please try again.' Block enforcement is bidirectional — checked in the POST handler before calling createThreadServer.

steps.title: Open New Thread
steps.notes: On 201: navigate to the new thread view. On 409: redirect to the existing thread (the response body contains the existing ThreadWithParticipants). The client service createThread() returns the thread in both cases — callers should differentiate via HTTP status if needed.

paths.label: 409 Duplicate
paths.detail: Thread already exists — redirect to it

flows.title: Open a Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks a thread row in the inbox, or navigates directly to a thread

steps.title: Fetch Thread Details
steps.tooltip: Returns ThreadWithParticipants: thread row joined with participant member details (name, avatar, slug) and my_unread_count. Returns 404 if the thread does not exist or user is not a participant (participant check is in getThreadByIdServer).

errorCases.condition: Thread not found or user not a participant

steps.title: Fetch Messages
steps.tooltip: Cursor-based pagination. Query params: ?cursor={messageId}&limit={1-100, default 50}. Returns { messages: MessageWithSender[], nextCursor: string | null }. Messages ordered newest-first. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Mark Thread Read
steps.tooltip: Resets unread_count to 0 and sets last_read_at to now on the user's message_thread_participants row. Called automatically on thread open. last_read_at is used by the other participant to determine the read receipt threshold.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Subscribe to Messages
steps.tooltip: useRealtimeMessages(threadId, userId) opens a Supabase Realtime channel subscribed to INSERT events on the messages table filtered by thread_id. Incoming messages from other users are prepended to the first page of the useMessages infinite query cache immediately. Messages from the current user are skipped — they are already handled by the optimistic update in useSendMessage. After prepending, the query is also invalidated to hydrate full sender data on the next fetch.

steps.title: Show Thread Skeleton
steps.tooltip: Rendered while useThread() or useMessages() isPending. Shows a skeleton header block and 6 alternating left/right bubble rows (3 left, 3 right) with avatar placeholders. Has role="status" aria-live="polite" and sr-only "Loading conversation". ComposeBar is rendered disabled during load.

steps.title: Show Thread Error State
steps.tooltip: Rendered when useThread() or useMessages() isError is true. Shows ErrorState banner variant with message "Something went wrong loading this conversation" and a Retry button that calls refetchThread() and refetchMessages() together. ComposeBar is rendered disabled.

steps.title: Render Collapsible Header
steps.tooltip: Thread context header above the message area. Renders thread-type-specific content: inquiry threads show participant avatar, name link (/member/{slug}), and a link to the listing ("About: Listing #{id}"); offer threads show an OfferBubble with accept/decline/counter actions if the current user is the seller and the offer is pending; direct and custom_request threads show participant avatar and name link. When collapsed, renders a slim bar showing TypeBadge + participant first name. Toggle is aria-expanded and aria-label aware.

steps.title: Render Thread View
steps.tooltip: Full thread detail page at /messages/[thread_id]. Composed of: CollapsibleHeader (thread context), a scrollable message area with MessageThread (chat bubbles, system/nudge nodes, listing/offer nodes, date separators), and ComposeBar at the bottom. On initial data load, scrolls to the bottom of the message area. useMarkRead() fires on mount to clear the unread count.

steps.title: Show Empty Thread State
steps.tooltip: Rendered when messages array has zero items after a successful load. Shows centered paragraph: "Send a message to get started". ComposeBar remains enabled so the user can send the first message.

steps.title: Show Archived/Closed Banner
steps.tooltip: Rendered when thread.status is 'archived' or 'closed'. Shows an InlineBanner (info variant) above the ComposeBar with label "This conversation is archived" or "This conversation is closed". ComposeBar is rendered with disabled=true so the user cannot send new messages.

steps.title: Load Older Messages
steps.tooltip: IntersectionObserver on a sentinel div pinned to the top of the message area. When the sentinel enters the viewport and hasNextPage is true, fetchNextPage() is called (useMessages infinite query). Scroll position is preserved after new pages append: previousScrollHeight is captured before fetch and the container.scrollTop is adjusted by the height diff after the new messages render.

flows.title: Send a Message
flows.trigger: User types and submits a message in an open thread

steps.title: Compose Message
steps.tooltip: Auto-expanding textarea at the bottom of the thread view. Grows up to 5 rows then scrolls. Enter submits the message; Shift+Enter inserts a newline. Send button is disabled when input is empty or a send is in-flight (aria-busy). Each keystroke calls onTyping() which triggers the Broadcast channel typing event (throttled to once per second). The + button opens an action menu with: 'Send Photo' (opens file picker for up to 4 images), 'Make an Offer' (inquiry threads only, buyer role only), and 'Share a Listing' (disabled — not yet built). Menu closes on outside click or Escape.

steps.title: Broadcast Typing
steps.tooltip: useTypingIndicator(threadId, userId) opens a Supabase Realtime Broadcast channel named 'typing-{threadId}'. When the user types, startTyping() sends { userId, threadId } on the 'typing' event. The hook receives incoming events and sets isOtherTyping=true, then auto-clears it after 3 seconds of inactivity via a debounced timeout. The current user's own events are filtered out by comparing payload.userId to currentUserId.

steps.title: Show Typing Indicator
steps.tooltip: Rendered below the message area when isOtherTyping is true. Shows the other participant's first name + animated three-dot indicator (e.g. 'Alex is typing...'). Uses role="status" aria-live="polite" for screen reader announcements. Auto-disappears after 3 seconds if no new typing event is received.

steps.title: Send Message
steps.tooltip: Body: { content, type? }. type defaults to 'text'. Runs participant check, block check, and safety filter (block → 422, redact → stored filtered, nudge → nudge system message appended, pass → as-is). Returns 201 MessageWithSender.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

errorCases.result: Returns 403 — cannot send to this user

steps.title: Apply Safety Filter
steps.tooltip: Processing order: block → redact → nudge → pass. Block: explicit/profane content → 422 rejected. Redact: PII (phone, email, credit card via Luhn) → replaced with [removed], original stored in original_content with is_filtered=true. Nudge: off-platform dealing or price negotiation → message passes but a nudge system message is inserted after it. Pass: no match → stored as-is.

steps.title: Update Thread Preview
steps.tooltip: createMessageServer() updates last_message_at and last_message_preview on the message_threads row, and increments unread_count on all other participants' message_thread_participants rows.

steps.title: Notify Recipient
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget: sent only when the recipient's unread_count transitions from 0 to 1 (i.e. this is their first unread message in the thread). Subject: 'New message from {senderName}'. Includes message preview (truncated at 200 chars) and a deep link to the thread. Template: src/features/email/templates/new-message.ts.
steps.notes: Dispatched inside a void async IIFE after the 201 response is already queued — never blocks the API. Checks notification_preferences.email.community_messages before sending (defaults to true if unset). Fetches recipient email via admin client. Silently catches all errors.

steps.title: In-App Notification
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget: dispatched for text-type messages only via the send-message API route (type === 'text'). Fetches sender name from members table, truncates message content to 100 chars for the notification body, then calls dispatchNotification() for each non-sender participant. type: 'new_message', link: /messages/{thread_id}. Runs in a separate void async IIFE after the email notification block — never blocks the API response.
steps.notes: Uses dispatchNotification() from src/features/notifications/utils/dispatch-notification.ts — fire-and-forget wrapper that silently catches errors. Nudge-type system messages are excluded (type check skips non-text). Image messages dispatch their own in-app notification from POST /api/messaging/threads/[thread_id]/upload — see the send-image-message flow. See notifications.json for the full notification center flow.

steps.title: Message Appears
steps.tooltip: useSendMessage uses optimistic updates: the message is prepended to the first page of the infinite query cache immediately on mutate, before the server responds. On success: input clears, textarea height resets, and focus returns to the textarea. On error: the optimistic message is rolled back via the onError context snapshot. The mutation settles by invalidating both the messages query and the threads list query.

steps.title: Show Nudge Message
steps.tooltip: When the safety filter returns nudge_off_platform or nudge_negotiation, the API inserts a second message with type='nudge' immediately after the user's message. MessageNode renders nudge-type messages as an InlineBanner (info variant) centered in the thread — not attributed to a sender. This guides the user toward platform-native flows (e.g. the offer system) without blocking their original message.

steps.title: Show New Message Pill
steps.tooltip: When a new message arrives via Realtime and the user is scrolled more than 150px from the bottom of the message area, a "New message" pill button appears at the bottom of the message viewport. Clicking it smooth-scrolls to the bottom and dismisses the pill. If the user is already near the bottom, the view auto-scrolls instead and the pill is never shown.

paths.label: Redact
paths.detail: PII removed — filtered content stored, original in audit trail

paths.label: Nudge
paths.detail: Off-platform or negotiation attempt — message passes, nudge appended

flows.title: Send an Image Message
flows.trigger: User taps + in the compose bar and selects Send Photo

steps.title: Open Action Menu
steps.tooltip: User taps the + button in the ComposeBar. A popover menu opens with role='menu': 'Send Photo' (HiCamera icon), 'Make an Offer' (inquiry threads, buyer role only), and 'Share a Listing' (disabled). Clicking 'Send Photo' calls handlePhotoSelect(), closes the menu, and programmatically clicks the hidden file input. Menu closes on outside click or Escape.

steps.title: Select Images from File Picker
steps.tooltip: Hidden <input type='file' accept='image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp,image/gif' multiple> opens the native file picker. On change, handleFileChange() validates: MIME type (JPEG/PNG/WebP/GIF only), file size (5MB max per file), and count (max 4 files — first 4 accepted, rest silently dropped with a count warning). Valid files are stored in selectedFiles state. Input value is reset so the same file can be re-selected.

steps.title: Show Validation Error
steps.tooltip:
If any file fails MIME or size validation: imageError state is set and a

renders below the ImagePreviewStrip with the error text (e.g. 'Invalid file type. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF' or 'One or more files exceed the 5MB limit'). No files are accepted. If count exceeds 4, first 4 are accepted and error reads 'Maximum 4 images per message. Only the first 4 were selected.'

steps.title: Show Image Preview Strip
steps.tooltip: ImagePreviewStrip renders above the compose bar textarea when selectedFiles is non-empty. Shows a horizontal thumbnail list using URL.createObjectURL() for each file (revoked on unmount). Each thumbnail has an 'X' remove button. A count indicator shows '{n}/4'. A send button (HiPaperAirplane icon) triggers handleSendImages(). Strip returns null when no files are selected.

steps.title: Tap Send in Preview Strip
steps.tooltip: User taps the send button in the ImagePreviewStrip. handleSendImages() calls sendImages(selectedFiles) via useSendImages mutation. The send button in the strip is disabled and aria-busy during the upload. The main textarea send button is also disabled while isUploadPending is true.

steps.title: Prepend Optimistic Message
steps.tooltip: useSendImages onMutate: cancels in-flight messages queries, snapshots previous state, then prepends an optimistic MessageWithSender (type: 'image', content: 'Sent a photo', metadata: null) to the first page of the useMessages infinite query cache. The optimistic message has a random UUID and a placeholder sender with empty name fields. On error: cache is rolled back via the snapshot context.

steps.title: Upload Images
steps.tooltip: multipart/form-data body: files[] (1–4 files). Auth check → participant check (403 if not participant) → block check (403 if recipient has blocked sender) → file count validation (max 4) → per-file MIME and size validation (422 on failure). For each file: scanImage() moderation → Sharp resize to 1200×1200 max (fit: inside, no upscale) → convert to WebP at 80% quality → upload to 'message-images' bucket at threads/{thread_id}/{message_id}/{uuid}.webp. On any upload failure, already-uploaded files are cleaned up. Calls createMessageServer() with type: 'image' and metadata: { images: ImageAttachment[] }. Returns 201 MessageWithSender.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

errorCases.result: Returns 403 — You cannot send messages to this user
Returns 400 — Too many files. Maximum is 4
Returns 422 — Invalid file type. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF
Returns 422 — Image failed moderation check. Already-uploaded files in the batch are deleted from storage.
Returns 500 — Failed to upload image. Already-uploaded files in the batch are deleted.

steps.title: Scan Image
steps.tooltip: scanImage(buffer) runs before each file is processed by Sharp. Currently a pass-through stub that always returns { passed: true } — will be wired to the real image scanner when issue #318 (platform-wide content moderation) lands. Returns { passed: boolean; reason?: string }.
steps.notes: Moderation is applied per-file on the original buffer before Sharp processing. If any file in a multi-file upload fails, the entire batch is rejected and any already-uploaded Storage files are cleaned up.

steps.title: Update Thread Preview
steps.tooltip: createMessageServer() sets last_message_preview to 'Sent a photo' (IMAGE_MESSAGE_PREVIEW) and updates last_message_at on the message_threads row. Increments unread_count on all other participants' message_thread_participants rows.

steps.title: Notify Recipient by Email
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget: sent only when the recipient's unread_count transitions from 0 to 1. Uses the newMessage email template with messagePreview: 'Sent a photo'. Subject: 'New message from {senderName}'. Deep link to the thread. Checks notification_preferences.email.community_messages before sending. Silently catches all errors.
steps.notes: Dispatched in a void async IIFE after the 201 response is queued — never blocks the API. Same email template as text messages (new-message.ts) — the preview text is 'Sent a photo' rather than message content.

steps.title: In-App Notification
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget: dispatched for each non-sender participant regardless of unread count. Calls dispatchNotification() with type: 'new_message', body: 'Sent a photo', link: /messages/{thread_id}. Runs in the same void async IIFE as the email notification — never blocks the API response.
steps.notes: Note: unlike the text send-message route which gates in-app notification on unread_count transition, the upload route dispatches in-app notifications unconditionally for all non-sender participants. This is the current implementation — may be aligned with the text path in a future update.

steps.title: Show Upload Error
steps.tooltip:
On useSendImages onError: the optimistic message is rolled back from the cache, selectedFiles state is preserved (user can retry), and the onError callback sets imageError state. The

renders the error message below the preview strip.

steps.title: Render Image Message
steps.tooltip: MessageThread renders type='image' messages via ImageNode. The optimistic message has metadata: null so renders a loading placeholder; on query invalidation and refetch, the real metadata ({ images: ImageAttachment[] }) hydrates and ImageNode renders the grid. Layout adapts to image count: 1 image = full-width; 2 images = side-by-side; 3–4 images = 2×2 grid. Each image is a <button> triggering the lightbox at that index. Uses next/image with fill and object-fit: cover.

steps.title: Open Image Lightbox
steps.tooltip: Tapping any image in an ImageNode calls onImageClick(index), which sets lightboxOpen=true and lightboxInitialIndex to the tapped index on MessageThread. ImageLightbox renders via ReactDOM.createPortal to #modal-root. Shows the tapped image full-screen (next/image fill, object-fit: contain). Multi-image messages show Prev/Next chevron buttons and a '{current} / {total}' counter. Escape key closes. Arrow keys navigate. Focus trap active — Tab cycles within the lightbox. Body scroll locked while open. On close, focus returns to the triggering image button.

flows.title: Receive Realtime Message
flows.trigger: Other participant sends a message in the open thread

steps.title: Receive Message Event
steps.tooltip: Supabase Realtime INSERT event fires on the messages table for the open thread_id channel. The hook prepends the incoming message to the first page of the useMessages cache. Sender data from the Realtime payload has placeholder name fields; a follow-up query invalidation hydrates the full sender profile on the next refetch. Deduplication: if the message ID already exists in the first page (from an optimistic insert by the current user), it is skipped.

steps.title: Scroll or Show Pill
steps.tooltip: On latestMessageId change: if the user is within 150px of the bottom (isNearBottom()), the thread area smooth-scrolls to the bottom. If the user is scrolled further up, the 'New message' pill appears instead to avoid interrupting their scroll position.

steps.title: Show Read Receipt
steps.tooltip: Below the last sent text message (identified by lastSentMessageIndex), a 'Read' label appears when otherParticipantLastReadAt is set and is newer than that message's created_at. The last_read_at value comes from the ThreadWithParticipants data returned by useThread() — it is set by PATCH /api/messaging/threads/{id}/read when the other participant opens the thread.

flows.title: Online Status Heartbeat
flows.trigger: Authenticated user has the app open

steps.title: Send Heartbeat
steps.tooltip: useOnlineStatus(enabled) fires PATCH /api/members/heartbeat on mount and then every 60 seconds. The request sets last_seen_at = now() on the members row for the authenticated user. Best-effort — failures are silently swallowed. Enabled is false for unauthenticated users.

steps.title: Show Online Indicator
steps.notes: isOnline(lastSeenAt) utility returns true if last_seen_at is within the last 5 minutes. A green dot indicator on participant avatars (thread header, inbox rows) is planned but not yet wired into the UI components. The heartbeat and isOnline() logic are built; the rendering layer is still outstanding.

flows.title: Archive a Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks Archive in the thread header or inbox row actions

steps.title: Archive Thread
steps.tooltip: Sets thread_status = 'archived' on the message_threads row. Archived threads are excluded from the default inbox view. Returns { success: true }. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

steps.title: Thread Removed
steps.notes: On success: optimistic removal from the active inbox list, toast 'Conversation archived'. Thread should reappear if the user opens an Archived filter tab. useArchiveThread mutation should invalidate ['messaging', 'threads'].

flows.title: Make an Offer
flows.trigger: Buyer clicks Make Offer on a listing detail page

steps.title: Open Offer Sheet
steps.tooltip: Seller clicks 'Make Offer' in the ComposeBar (available for inquiry threads when currentUserRole is 'buyer'). Sets offerSheetMode='create' and opens the OfferSheet bottom drawer. The sheet shows listing title, price, the 70% minimum floor, and an amount input prefilled at 80% of listing price.

steps.title: Validate Offer Amount
steps.tooltip: Client-side validation using validateOfferAmount(amountCents, listingPriceCents) before submitting. Minimum is 70% of listing price (OFFER_MIN_PERCENTAGE = 0.70). Shows inline error if below floor.

steps.title: Submit Offer
steps.tooltip: createOfferServer() validates: listing is active, buyer is not the seller, amount >= 70% of price, seller is valid; expires stale offers; creates offer thread + offer row in one operation. Returns the created Offer.

steps.title: Notify Seller
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget email to seller after offer creation. Subject: 'New offer on {listingTitle}'. Includes offer amount and a CTA linking to the offer thread. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'received').
steps.notes: Dispatched inside a void async IIFE after createOfferServer() succeeds — never blocks the API response. Uses getOfferEmailContext() to fetch sender name and listing title. Checks recipient's notification_preferences.email.community_messages before sending. Silently catches all errors.

steps.title: Offer Thread Opens
steps.tooltip: createOfferServer() creates a thread of type='offer' as part of the offer creation. handleOfferCreated() receives the new offer and navigates to /messages/{newOffer.thread_id}, opening the offer thread where the offer appears as an offer_node message rendered by OfferBubble.

steps.title: Seller Reviews Offer
steps.tooltip: CollapsibleHeader shows an OfferBubble for offer-type threads when offer data is loaded. Accept and Decline action buttons are rendered to the seller when offer.status is 'pending' (isSeller check via participant role). Counter-offer opens the OfferSheet in 'counter' mode. Accept/decline/counter trigger useOfferActions mutations with optimistic status updates. OfferBubble handles all 5 statuses: pending, accepted, declined, countered, expired.

steps.title: Accept Offer
steps.tooltip: Seller-only, pending-only. Calls acceptOfferServer() which sets offer status to 'accepted' and inserts a system message into the thread. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending.

steps.title: Notify Buyer Accepted
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget email to buyer after seller accepts the offer. Subject: 'Your offer on {listingTitle} was accepted!'. CTA label: 'Continue to Checkout'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'accepted').
steps.notes: Dispatched inside a void async IIFE after acceptOfferServer() succeeds. Sends to offer.buyer_id. Uses getOfferEmailContext() for sender name (the seller) and listing title. Checks community_messages preference. Silently catches all errors.

steps.title: Decline Offer
steps.tooltip: Seller-only, pending-only. Calls declineOfferServer() which sets offer status to 'declined' and inserts a system message into the thread. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending.

steps.title: Notify Buyer Declined
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget email to buyer after seller declines the offer. Subject: 'Your offer on {listingTitle} was declined'. CTA label: 'View Thread'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'declined').
steps.notes: Dispatched inside a void async IIFE after declineOfferServer() succeeds. Sends to offer.buyer_id. Uses getOfferEmailContext() for sender name (the seller) and listing title. Checks community_messages preference. Silently catches all errors.

steps.title: Counter Offer
steps.tooltip: Seller-only. Calls counterOfferServer() which marks the original offer as 'countered' and creates a new offer row with buyer/seller roles swapped and parent_offer_id set. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending; 400 if amountCents is missing or below floor.

errorCases.condition: Counter amount below 70% floor or zero

steps.title: Notify Buyer Countered
steps.tooltip: Fire-and-forget email to the original offerer after a counter is made. Subject: 'Counter offer on {listingTitle}'. CTA label: 'View Counter Offer'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'countered').
steps.notes: Dispatched inside a void async IIFE after counterOfferServer() succeeds. Note: counterOfferServer() swaps buyer/seller roles on the new offer row — the email recipient is offer.seller_id on the returned counter offer, which is the original buyer. Uses getOfferEmailContext() for sender name (the seller who countered) and listing title. Checks community_messages preference. Silently catches all errors.

steps.title: Expire Stale Offers
steps.notes: Vercel cron at GET /api/cron/offers-expiry runs daily (Hobby plan). expirePendingOffersServer() expires pending offers after 24h and accepted offers after 4h checkout window. Inserts system message 'This offer has expired.' into each offer thread. On Pro plan, increase frequency to every 15 minutes.

flows.title: Block a User from Thread
flows.trigger: User clicks Block in a thread header or participant context menu

steps.title: Click Block User
steps.notes: Block entry point from a messaging thread — e.g., kebab menu in the thread header with a 'Block this user' option. Client calls blockMember(memberId) from src/features/messaging/services/blocks.ts. Thread UI is Phase 3; this step is planned alongside it.

steps.title: Create Block Record
steps.tooltip: Body: { memberId }. Auth check via Supabase session. Self-block guard (400). Calls blockMemberServer() which inserts into member_blocks. Returns 201 MemberBlock. Returns 409 if already blocked.

errorCases.condition: memberId is missing from request body

steps.title: Show Block Feedback
steps.notes: On 201 or 409: show success toast (e.g., 'Blocked — You can no longer receive messages from this user.'). Thread should archive or close. The blocked user will receive 403 on any further message sends to this thread. Invalidate ['blocks', userId] so the account settings blocked-members list stays in sync. See member-block-management.json for the full block management flow.

steps.title: Show Block Error
steps.notes: On 400 or 500: show error toast (e.g., 'Could not block this user. Please try again.'). Thread remains open.

flows.title: Unread Count in Nav
flows.trigger: User loads any page — nav header subscribes to unread count changes

steps.title: Fetch Unread Count
steps.tooltip: Sums unread_count across all active participant rows. useUnreadCount() has no polling interval — the count stays fresh via useRealtimeUnreadCount, which invalidates this query key on any UPDATE to the user's message_thread_participants rows. Query key: ['messages', 'unread-count'].

steps.title: Subscribe to Unread Changes
steps.tooltip: useRealtimeUnreadCount(userId) subscribes to UPDATE events on message_thread_participants filtered by member_id. On any change, it invalidates both ['messages', 'unread-count'] and ['messages', 'threads'] query keys, triggering a refetch of the badge count and thread list. This replaces the previous polling-based approach.

steps.title: Render Unread Badge
steps.notes: Nav inbox icon badge showing total unread count. Should be driven by useUnreadCount() hook. Badge should be hidden at 0 and cap display at 99+. Needs to invalidate when markThreadRead succeeds.
